content:
Key Findings

  • Burnley Borough Council's Planning & Transportation department spent £58.4M between 2021/22 and 2025/26
  • This represents 16.4% of the council's total £355.2M expenditure
  • Planning & Transportation is the second-largest spending department after an unnamed department with £137.5M
  • Environmental Services spent £35.7M, just £0.4M more than Legal & Democratic Services
  • Chief Executive & Corporate Services accounted for £10.0M, the smallest departmental spend

Planning & Transportation: Burnley's Second-Largest Department

Burnley Borough Council's Planning & Transportation department spent £58.4M between 2021/22 and 2025/26. This substantial investment reflects the department's critical role in managing the borough's infrastructure and development. The £58.4M spend represents 16.4% of the council's total £355.2M expenditure, making it the second-largest department after an unnamed department with £137.5M.

The department's budget is significantly larger than other key services. Environmental Services, for example, spent £35.7M, while Legal & Democratic Services allocated £35.3M. This highlights the priority placed on planning and transportation initiatives in Burnley. The department's responsibilities likely include managing transport networks, planning permissions, and infrastructure projects, all of which are essential for the borough's growth and development.

Departmental Spending Breakdown

To understand the context of the Planning & Transportation department's spend, it is useful to compare it with other departments. The table below shows the spending breakdown for Burnley Borough Council's top departments. The unnamed department with £137.5M accounts for 38.7% of total spending, dwarfing all other departments. Planning & Transportation's £58.4M is still substantial, representing 16.4% of the total.

Rank Department Amount % of Total
1 Unnamed £137.5M 38.7%
2 Planning & Transportation £58.4M 16.4%
3 Environmental Services £35.7M 10.1%
4 Legal & Democratic £35.3M 9.9%
5 Regeneration & Housing £26.5M 7.5%

The data shows a clear prioritisation of the unnamed department, followed by Planning & Transportation. Environmental Services and Legal & Democratic Services have similar budgets, both just over £35M. Regeneration & Housing, with £26.5M, also plays a significant role in the council's spending priorities. The Chief Executive & Corporate Services department, with £10.0M, has the smallest budget, reflecting its more administrative role compared to service-delivering departments.

What You Can Do

  • Submit a Freedom of Information request to Burnley Borough Council about the unnamed department's spending, which accounts for 38.7% of the total budget
  • Attend council committee meetings to learn more about planning and transportation projects in Burnley
  • Check the council's published contracts register to see which suppliers are working with the Planning & Transportation department
  • Write to your local councillor about the prioritisation of the Planning & Transportation department's budget
  • Visit aidoge.co.uk to explore the full spending data and analyse other departments' expenditures
This article is based on Burnley Borough Council's published spending data covering 2021/22 to 2025/26. All figures are drawn directly from transactions published under the Local Government Transparency Code. Analysis by AI DOGE.
